div1.entryFree:
Fine, vb. 1) to punish: to f. the faults, Meas. II, 2, 40. why would he for the momentary trick be perdurably --d? III, 1, 115. the nobles hath he --d for ancient quarrels, R2 II, 1, 247 (here undoubtedly meaning a pecuniary punishment).
2) to fix as the sum to be paid: I have --d these bones of mine for ransom, H5 IV, 7, 72.
3) to refine, to purify: time's office is to f. the hate of foes, Lucr. 936 (or is it == to end?). to f. his title with some shows of truth, H5 I, 2, 72 (so the spurious Q and some M. Edd.; Ff find; see Find, def. 2).
